workflow.daily-use.label: Daily Workflow — /plan → /autopilot → verify → /compact
workflow.daily-use.description: How you actually work: one terminal orchestrates with /plan, a second terminal runs /autopilot, then back to the first to verify and merge

steps.caption: Open your first terminal. Tell Claude what you want to build, in plain English.
Run /plan. Claude breaks your task down before writing any code.
A specialist sub-task figures out every file to change, in what order, and how to undo it if something goes wrong.
Two more sub-tasks pressure-test the plan — one challenges the assumptions, one grades it against engineering rules. The plan is revised until both pass.
Open a second terminal. Paste the /autopilot command /plan handed you. It runs the plan on its own while you do other things.
A QA sub-task audits every change and re-checks until the work is clean. No "should work" — only verified.
Switch back to your first terminal. Claude waits for you to confirm before pushing anything.
When you're done, run /compact. It throws away the noise and keeps your next conversation lean.

command.autopilot.label: /autopilot — Autonomous Multi-Phase Orchestrator
command.autopilot.description: Plan → Implement → QA → Commit, end-to-end with API retry + circuit breaker

steps.caption: User invokes /autopilot with a task description
Phase 1: dispatch safe-planner for work-unit plan
Phase 2: implementation work units in parallel where safe
Phase 3: QA loop — audit + fix until clean

command.bug.label: /bug — Trace, Diagnose, Fix, Validate
command.bug.description: 4-phase systematic debugging, stops at 3+ failed fixes

steps.caption: Dispatch bug-fix: trace flow → identify root cause
Apply narrow fix at root cause
qa-agent confirms symptom gone, no regressions

command.qa-loop.label: /qa-loop — Iterative Audit-and-Fix
command.qa-loop.description: Revision gate: audit → fix → re-audit until clean (max 3 iterations)

steps.caption: Revision gate: loop or escalate per gates.md

command.plan.label: /plan — Plan with Brainstorm + Principles Verification
command.plan.description: safe-planner → brainstorm critique → rubric grading → revision loop

steps.caption: Revision gate: one retry pass if either critique flags issues

command.autopilot-merge.label: /autopilot-merge — Sequential `--no-ff` merge of every completed `autopilot/*` branch in this repo
command.autopilot-merge.description: Sequential `--no-ff` merge of every completed `autopilot/*` branch in this repo onto a target branch (default `dev`). Preserves per-run commit history with one merge commit per autopilot run. Pauses on conflicts for human resolution. Does NOT auto-clean worktrees or branches.

command.brainstorm.label: /brainstorm — Dispatch the `brainstorm` agent to apply first principles, inversion, and second
command.brainstorm.description: Dispatch the `brainstorm` agent to apply first principles, inversion, and second-order thinking. Surfaces the real problem, not just the presented one.

command.goal.label: /goal — Goal-driven autonomous loop: write .claude/GOAL.md (goal + acceptance criteria),
command.goal.description: Goal-driven autonomous loop: write .claude/GOAL.md (goal + acceptance criteria), then implement → verify each criterion with fresh evidence (live-test when UI/live behavior is in scope) → update status → repeat until all criteria pass or blocked. Use when the user states a goal with autonomy + completion phrasing: "until perfect", "do it all yourself", "fix without me", "live test until all is perfect", "brainstorm and fix without me".

command.redesign.label: /redesign — Redesign an existing UI through collaborative brainstorming, mockup generation,
command.redesign.description: Redesign an existing UI through collaborative brainstorming, mockup generation, implementation, and visual verification. Use when the user says "redesign this page/component", "same UI redesign exercise", "3 options on an html page", or "brainstorm options and let me pick".
